sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to fresh or dried kava products that are used to prepare a beverage when mixed with potable water, intended for human consumption, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard. The standard does not apply to the final kava beverage as such, or kava products used for medicinal purposes, or as ingredients in foods or other tradable product, or for any other purposes.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Kava products are derived from selected parts of the Noble cultivar of the kava plant, Piper methysticum G. Forst. in the family Piperaceae. The parts of the kava plant used to produce kava products may include : a) peeled, fresh and/or dried rhizomes, basal stems (up to the first node on each kava branch); and b) fresh and/or dried roots. Upper stems, leaves, peelings (bark), and extraction residues are excluded.

sections.title: Raw materials
sections.text: Kava plants used as raw material for kava products shall be a Noble variety. The Noble variety shall be confirmed using their morphological characteristics. Kava of the wild, Piper wichmannii and Two-day (Tudei) varieties are excluded. The following list is non-exhaustive and includes examples of vernacular terms used to describe some Noble varieties in the various regions: i. Federated States of Micronesia: Rahmwahnger; ii. Fiji: Damu, Dokobana loa, Dokobana vula, Yonolulu, Loa kasa balavu, Loa kasa leka, Matakaro balavu, Matakaro leka, Qila balavu, Qila leka, Vula kasa balavu, Vula kasa leka, Yalu; iii. Hawaii: Hanakapi’ai, Hiwa, Honokane Iki, Kumakua, Mahakea, Mapulehu, Moi, Nene, Opihikao, Pana’ewa, Papa ‘Ele‘ele, Papa ‘Ele‘ele Pu ‘upu‘u, Papa kea; iv. Papua New Guinea: Kau kupwe; v. Samoa: Ava La’au, Ava Le’a, Ava Loa, Ava Talo, Ava Mumu; vi. Solomon Islands: Feo, Tahu, Temo; vii. Tonga: Kava Lekahina, Kava ‘Akauhina, Kava Lekakula, Kava ‘Akaukula, Kava Fulufulu, Kava Valu, Kava Kofe; viii. Vanuatu: Ahouia, Amon, Asiyai, Bir Kar, Bir Sul, Biyaj, Borogoru, Borogu, Gorgor, Gegusug, Gevemea, Gewiswisket, Kelai, Leay, Melmel, Melomelo, Miela, Nagamiwok, Olitao, Palarasul, Palasa, Palimet, Pia, Poivota, Pualiu, Puariki, Sese, Silese, Urukara.

sections.title: Production and post-harvest handling
sections.text: Kava plants should be cultivated using good agricultural practices. The roots, rhizomes and/or basal stems are harvested and washed and peeled when tissues have been exposed to sunlight. They may be sliced, dried or fresh. Dried kava may also be ground into powder.

sections.title: Quality criteria
sections.text: Kava products shall be: • of known Noble kava varieties, based on meeting the criteria of ‘Absorbance of acetonic extracts’ (less than or equal to 0.9 absorbance units) and with a suitable total kavalactone; • have no intentional adulteration; • free of leaves, bark, and/or stems; • practically free from pests; • practically free from damage caused by pests; • free of visible mould; • free from soil and foreign materials; and • free from foreign odour.

sections.title: Packaging and storage
sections.text: Kava products shall be packaged in such a manner as to safeguard the hygienic and organoleptic quality of the products. Kava products shall be stored in such a manner as to avoid pest access or harborage, protected from contamination, and under conditions of temperature and humidity that minimize deterioration and minimize mould growth. Fresh kava products shall be quick frozen and maintained at < -18 0C. Dried kava products shall be stored in a sealed container and the moisture content shall not exceed 12 percent.

sections.text: If prepared from dried kava, the powder is mixed with potable water and may be filtered prior to consumption. If prepared from fresh kava, the ground or macerated kava is mixed with potable water and may be filtered prior to consumption.

sections.title: Name of the product
sections.text: The name of the food shall be “fresh kava” or “dried kava” together with the part of the kava plant from which the kava product is derived. Kava products shall have a clear marking that they are Noble kava. Optionally, the name of the variety(ies) of kava plant from which the kava product is derived may be stated.

sections.title: Instructions for use
sections.text: The label of each container of k ava products shall have a clear, conspicuous and easily -readable message, which includes the following points: a) the statement “Steps to prepare the kava beverage” or a similar statement followed by specifically numbered actions to prepare the kava beverage; and b) the first action referred to in Section 7.3(a) should read “Use only potable water to prepare the kava beverage” or a similar statement.

sentences.original: This standard applies to fresh or dried kava products that are used to prepare a beverage when mixed with potable water, intended for human consumption, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

pages.title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR KAVA PRODUCTS FOR USE AS A BEVERAGE WHEN MIXED WITH WATER
pages.summary: Página apresenta título principal e código padrão como cabeçalho. Não há seções, subtítulos ou texto interno para mapear nesta página.
Página apresenta apenas texto corrido introdutório, sem títulos ou seções estruturais detectadas para mapeamento hierárquico.

Página contém seções principais numeradas de 1 a 3 com subtítulos claramente numerados. Texto interno associado a cada título/subtítulo conforme sua posição natural no texto. Não há indicação clara de continuidade de estruturas entre páginas.
Página apresenta seções principais numeradas de 3.5 até 7 com textos associados claros. Inclui uma subseção 7.1 sob LABELLING. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas.

A página apresenta seções principais 7 LABELLING e 8 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING. A seção 7 é continuação da página anterior, com subseções 7.2 a 7.5 detalhadas, cada uma com texto específico. A seção 8 inicia nesta página. Não há continuidade indicada para após esta página.

pages.title: CXS 336R-2020
pages.summary: A página contém duas seções principais: 'NOTES', que lista diversas referências da FAO e WHO com texto detalhado, e uma seção secundária indicando os membros da Comissão do Codex Alimentarius na região da América do Norte e Pacífico Sudoeste, com link para o website. Ambas as seções são apresentadas totalmente nesta página, sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas.
